prompt:
Refactor: extract the Zed editor configuration from `home/desktop.nix` into its own dedicated module `home/zed.nix`, and import it. This is a pure move/no-behavior-change refactor of the config this PR added.

## What to do
1. Create a new file `home/zed.nix` as a standard home-manager module. Follow the existing style of the other single-purpose modules in `home/` (e.g. `home/mako.nix`, `home/waybar.nix` — they start with a minimal function head like `{ pkgs, ... }:` or `{ config, pkgs, ... }:` and contain one `programs.*`/`services.*` block). The module's function head should take exactly the arguments the Zed block needs (likely just `{ ... }:` or `{ pkgs, ... }:`).
2. MOVE the entire `programs.zed-editor = { ... };` block (enable, extensions, and the full userSettings — theme, vim_mode, relative_line_numbers, buffer font, format_on_save, tab_size, minimap, telemetry, git inline_blame, nixd LSP + nixfmt languages, and the Claude Code AI comment) out of `home/desktop.nix` and into `home/zed.nix`, verbatim (no settings changes).
3. In `home/desktop.nix`, add `./zed.nix` to the existing `imports` list (which currently contains `./dev.nix` and `./hyprland.nix`). Keep the existing `# Zed is installed declaratively via programs.zed-editor below.` comment near the IDEs list updated/accurate — e.g. change it to point at the new module (`# Zed is configured declaratively in ./zed.nix`) or remove it if it no longer makes sense in context.

## IMPORTANT — what STAYS in desktop.nix
- The dark-mode fix `dconf.settings."org/gnome/desktop/interface".color-scheme = "prefer-dark";` (and the existing `org/freedesktop/appearance` block) must REMAIN in `home/desktop.nix`. That is a system-wide appearance/portal setting affecting all GTK/portal apps, NOT Zed-specific. Do not move it into zed.nix.
- Do not move any other desktop settings (kitty, fuzzel, gtk theming, mimeApps, pointerCursor, etc.). Only the `programs.zed-editor` block moves.

## Acceptance
- `home/zed.nix` exists and contains the full `programs.zed-editor` config; `home/desktop.nix` no longer contains a `programs.zed-editor` block but DOES still contain the dconf dark-mode settings and imports `./zed.nix`.
- Config still evaluates: run `nix flake check` or build the relevant homeConfiguration target (inspect flake.nix outputs) and fix any evaluation errors before pushing. A missing import or wrong function-head args will fail evaluation — make sure it builds.
- Run `nix fmt` (nixfmt-tree) so both files are formatted.
- No behavior change to the Zed settings themselves — this is just relocating them.
- Follow CLAUDE.md: no Co-Authored-By, no mention of Claude/AI/LLM in the commit message or PR text. Commit message focused, e.g. 'home: move Zed config into its own module (home/zed.nix)'.

Push to this existing PR branch (#595).
